name: Terrateam GitOps Terraform API
what_it_does: GitHub-native GitOps platform for Terraform and OpenTofu with REST API — enabling plan/apply workflows via PR comments, drift detection, cost estimation, and policy enforcement, with the entire Terraform workflow living in GitHub Actions without separate infrastructure.
use_cases: Triggering Terraform plan/apply via GitHub PR comments (atlantis-style workflow)
Querying Terraform run history and drift detection results via API
Integrating Terrateam run status into custom dashboards or Slack notifications
Managing workspace configurations and directives programmatically
Monitoring infrastructure drift across multiple Terraform workspaces
not_for: Non-GitHub VCS users (Terrateam is GitHub-first; GitLab support limited)
Teams not using Terraform or OpenTofu
Complex multi-cloud governance requiring Spacelift or Scalr features
best_when: A team already deep in GitHub wants Terraform GitOps workflow with PR-based approvals, cost estimation, and drift detection without running separate Atlantis infrastructure.
avoid_when: You use GitLab, Bitbucket, or need advanced governance features beyond PR-based workflow.

auth.notes: GitHub OAuth2 for authentication — tied to GitHub identity. API token from Terrateam settings. GitHub App installation required. API scope tied to GitHub repository permissions. No fine-grained API scopes beyond GitHub permissions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited runs, unlimited users for open-source repos
pricing.paid_tiers: Business from $50/month for private repos; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Free for open-source repos. Affordable for private repos. US-based startup. GitHub App distribution. Cost estimation via Infracost integration. Drift detection. OPA policy integration. Alternative to self-hosted Atlantis. Growing community.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; GitHub API limits are the primary constraint

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for run history, workspace configs, and drift results. GitHub-native auth. Webhook for run events. Terraform workflow happens in GitHub Actions — API is for querying results, not triggering (use PR comments for triggering). GitHub App required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Run triggering is event-driven (PR comments). Run reads idempotent. Workspace config management idempotent. Drift detection runs are periodic.
known_agent_gotchas: Workflow is PR-comment-driven, not API-driven — API is for querying results
GitHub App must be installed on all repos before API access
GitHub API rate limits affect Terrateam performance on large repos
Terraform execution happens in GitHub Actions — bring your own compute

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. GitHub OAuth2 auth. US data residency. Terraform state stays in your own backend — Terrateam doesn't store it. GitHub App permissions scoped per repo. Cloud secrets stay in GitHub Actions secrets.
